result:
Done — the spec is fully satisfied. All three missing operations are implemented in `CsvRow.calr`, the project builds cleanly, and behavior is verified end-to-end.

What I added to `src/CsvRow.calr` (leaving `NeedsQuoting` untouched):

- **`EscapeField`** — calls `NeedsQuoting`; if quoting is needed it doubles internal quotes with `(replace field "\"" "\"\"")` and wraps the result in quotes, otherwise returns the field verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** — takes `List<str>`, walks it with a while loop, escapes each field via `EscapeField`, and joins with commas; the empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** — returns `List<str>` and declares the `mut` effect (it pushes into a local list). The empty line returns one empty field. Otherwise it scans character-by-character: a field opening with `"` is consumed until the un-doubled closing quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal quote; commas/CR/LF inside are content), unquoted fields run to the next comma or end of line, and a trailing comma appends a final empty field.

Two things came up along the way:

- The first build failed because I wrote the list type as `[str]`, which Calor compiles to `string[]` — the correct spelling for the pinned `List<string>` surface is `List<str>` in both parameter and return positions.
- After the clean build I verified behavior by compiling a throwaway console runner (in a temp dir, deleted afterward — no test files added to the project) against the built `Src.dll`. It checked all escaping cases, the empty-line/lone-comma/trailing-comma parses, doubled-quote handling, and `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields` round-trips over tricky inputs (embedded commas, quotes, CR/LF, empty fields). Everything passed: `ALL PASS`.

Budget used: 2 of 10 build cycles for the project itself, plus one scratch verification run.
